SELECT
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."gross_amount"), $4) AS total_revenue,
      COUNT(*)::text AS total_transactions,
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."quantity"), $5)::text AS total_quantity,
      COUNT(DISTINCT "sales_records"."product_id")::text AS unique_products,
      COUNT(DISTINCT "sales_records"."location_id")::text AS unique_outlets
    FROM "sales_records"
    INNER JOIN "loca

SELECT
      "products"."name" AS category_name,
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."gross_amount"), $4) AS revenue,
      COUNT(*)::text AS transactions,
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."quantity"), $5)::text AS quantity,
      COALESCE(AVG("sales_records"."gross_amount"), $6) AS avg_value
    FROM "sales_records"
    INNER JOIN "locations" ON "sales_records"."location_id" = "locations"."id"

SELECT
      "products"."name" AS product_name,
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."gross_amount"), $5) AS revenue,
      COUNT(*)::text AS transactions,
      COALESCE(SUM("sales_records"."quantity"), $6)::text AS quantity
    FROM "sales_records"
    INNER JOIN "locations" ON "sales_records"."location_id" = "locations"."id"
    INNER JOIN "products" ON "sales_records"."product_id" = "products"."

SELECT state, pg_catalog.to_char(state_change, $1::pg_catalog.text) AS state_change
                FROM pg_stat_activity
                    WHERE backend_type OPERATOR(pg_catalog.=) $2::pg_catalog.text
                    AND pid OPERATOR(pg_catalog.!=) pg_catalog.pg_backend_pid()
                    AND usename OPERATOR(pg_catalog.!=) $3::pg_catalog.name

SELECT
    pg_database_size(datname) AS db_size,
    datid
FROM
    pg_stat_database
WHERE datname IN (
    SELECT datname
    FROM pg_database
    WHERE datconnlimit != -2
    AND datname <> 'postgres'
    AND NOT datistemplate
    ORDER BY oid
    LIMIT 10
)

-- We export stats for 10 non-system databases. Without this limit it is too
-- easy to abuse the system by creating lots of databases.

SELECT pg_catalog.pg_database_size(datname) AS db_size,
  deadlocks,
  tup_inserted AS inserted,
  tup_updated AS updated,
  tup_deleted AS deleted,
  datname,
  datid
FROM pg_catalog.pg_stat_database
WHERE datname IN (
  SELECT datname FROM pg_database
  -- Igno
select count(*) from pg_stat_replication where application_name != $1
select count(*) from pg_stat_activity where backend_type = $1
select "id", "expires_at", "token", "created_at", "updated_at", "ip_address", "user_agent", "user_id" from "session" where "session"."token" = $1
select "id", "name", "email", "email_verified", "image", "created_at", "updated_at", "role", "banned", "ban_reason", "ban_expires", "user_type" from "user" where "user"."id" = $1

-- NOTE: This is the "internal" / "machine-readable" version. This outputs the
-- working set size looking back 1..60 minutes, labeled with the number of
-- minutes.

SELECT
  x::pg_catalog.text AS duration_seconds,
  COALESCE(neon.approximate_working_set_size_seconds(x), 0) AS size
FROM (SELECT generate_series * 60 AS x FROM generate_series(1, 60)) AS t (x)

SELECT setting::pg_catalog.int4 AS max_cluster_size FROM pg_catalog.pg_settings WHERE name = 'neon.max_cluster_size'
SELECT bucket_le, value FROM neon.neon_perf_counters WHERE metric = 'getpage_wait_seconds_bucket'

SELECT COALESCE(pg_catalog.sum(active_time), $1)::pg_catalog.float8 AS total_active_time,
            COALESCE(pg_catalog.sum(sessions), $2)::pg_catalog.int8 AS total_sessions
        FROM pg_catalog.pg_stat_database
        WHERE datname NOT IN (
                $3,
                $4,
                $5
            )

WITH c AS (SELECT pg_catalog.jsonb_object_agg(metric, value) jb FROM neon.neon_perf_counters)

SELECT d.* FROM pg_catalog.jsonb_to_record((SELECT jb FROM c)) AS d(
